<summary>Agent "Line-review outline subsystem" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>I have now read all four outline files end-to-end, the seed prompt path in `_call_outline`, the enrichment-residual seam, the run's outline JSON, and the seed-outliner's reasoning trace. Findings below.

**Task: line-by-line review of the OUTLINE subsystem for RACE-limiting defects.**

---

## Ranked findings — OUTLINE subsystem

### 1. The outline data model cannot express hierarchy — root cause of the 12 wall-of-text H2 sections (Readability 0.3981)
- **File/lines:** `src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:1216-1238` (`SectionPlan`), `:1616-1641` (`OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET`), `:966-974` (`_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM`)
- **Quotes:** `class SectionPlan: title: str … focus: str # one-sentence focus statement … ev_ids: list[str]` — that is the *entire* structural vocabulary. The facet prompt asks only for `"title": a SHORT, SPECIFIC, topical section heading … at most 8 words` and `"focus": one sentence`. The skeleton addendum constrains 4 roles and order but zero depth.
- **Problem:** The planner literally has no field in which to plan sub-structure, so compose receives 12 flat plans (several with 60–180 ev_ids) and renders each as one monolithic ~500-word paragraph. The scored report has **zero `###` subsections, zero tables, zero lists** — this is the largest single gap to leader reports and maps directly onto the weakest RACE dimension.
- **Fix:** Add `subsections: list[dict]` (each `{title, focus, ev_ids}`) to `SectionPlan` (positional-append-safe, like `archetype` was). Extend `OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET` to require 2–4 sub-facets for any section assigned more than a configured ev_id threshold, and an optional per-section `layout_hint` (`"prose" | "comparison_table" | "enumerated_findings"`) the planner sets when the section's evidence is a set of parallel quantitative studies (task-72's empirical sections are exactly this shape). Compose then renders `###` headings and tables from the plan — an upstream structural change, no post-generation filtering.
- **RACE impact:** Readability ↑↑ (largest expected single gain), Comprehensiveness ↑ (subsection headings surface facets the judge can see), Insight ↔.
- **Infra:** in-govkit; plain names (`subsections`, `layout_hint`); thresholds via resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented in the outline design doc; checkpoint-safe (`_plan_to_dict` already serializes unknown dataclass fields via `dataclasses.asdict`).

### 2. ~40% of the corpus is dumped into a residual "Additional Corroborated Findings" section rendered AFTER Conclusions
- **File/lines:** `src/polaris_graph/generator/weighted_enrichment.py:1353` (`_ENRICHMENT_RESIDUAL_TITLE = "Additional Corroborated Findings"`), `multi_section_generator.py:10718` (`plans.extend(_wfe_plans)` — appended last), run artifact `outputs/faithoff_t72/multi_section_outline.json` (residual plan carries ~430 ev_ids and `"focus": "Additional Corroborated Findings"` — the focus field is just the title, so the writer gets no analytical directive for the largest section in the report).
- **Problem:** The thematic outline assigned ~640 distinct ev_ids; everything else routed to the keep-all residual, which renders as a grab-bag *after* "Conclusions and Research Gaps" (then "Limitations" after that). A literature review that ends Conclusions → misc-findings-dump → Limitations reads broken to the RACE judge, and the internal-sounding title leaks pipeline vocabulary into the judged artifact.
- **Fix:** (a) Order by structural role at plan assembly: residual/enrichment plans insert BEFORE the synthesis/conclusion roles (the skeleton addendum already names the roles; use them for placement). (b) Split the residual through the already-existing deterministic theme clusterer (`outline_agent.py:2096 _derive_theme_coverage_sections` + `_titlecase_terms:2075`) so it becomes 2–3 *titled thematic* sections with corpus-derived names and real focus sentences instead of one 430-ev dump. (c) Give the residual a real `focus` directive.
- **RACE impact:** Readability ↑, Instruction-Following ↑ (document reads as the requested literature review), Comprehensiveness ↔ (nothing dropped — pure placement).
- **Infra:** in-govkit; descriptive titles from corpus terms (no casual names); cluster thresholds already env-seated — move to resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented; placement is deterministic and checkpoint-safe.

### 3. Instruction-following: the task's "high-quality, English-language journal articles" constraint is planned by the outliner, then overridden downstream
- **File/lines:** run artifact `reasoning_trace.jsonl` seed-outline call (deepseek-v4-pro): *"The user's request says 'Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.' So we should only include evidence IDs that correspond to journal articles… The safest is to include only T1 and T2"* — vs. `OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET:1636`: `"a lower-tier source that carries a real claim STILL earns a place at its honest weight"`, and the compose-stage keep-all routing that assigns every basket regardless. Final bibliography: **T5+T6+T7+UNKNOWN = 65 of 147 entries** (e.g. `[100] 7 Best Medical Transcriptionist Job Tips — phoenixvirtualstaff.com (tier T6)`).
- **Problem:** The seed planner correctly reads the task's *explicit source-type instruction* and tries to honor it; the facet prompt's generic tier language plus the route-all residual then put T6 blogs on the citation surface anyway. This is a direct, judge-visible instruction violation (the RQ is quoted verbatim in the prompt the judge scores against).
- **Fix:** Derive a `source_eligibility` field at outline seed time (from the RQ, mechanical keyword detection of explicit source-type constraints — "journal articles", "academic research", "peer-reviewed"), carry it on the workspace, and have the residual/enrichment routing honor it for *citation assignment* (ineligible rows stay in the pool and disclosures, never on a section's `ev_ids`). This is upstream assignment, not a post-generation filter, and it enforces the *user's own instruction*, not a quality verdict. (This is the outline-side generalization of the Rank12 source-eligibility firewall already proven at 43.3%→72.8% tier compliance.)
- **RACE impact:** Instruction-Following ↑↑, Readability ↑ (references list stops advertising blogs), FACT accuracy unharmed.
- **Infra:** in-govkit; plain name `source_eligibility`; detection patterns + tier map via resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented; workspace field checkpoints with the existing `build_cp4_payload`.

### 4. The gap checklist audits only coverage/density — nothing ever drives the fully-built `split` op, so 11 agent turns produced near-zero structural work
- **File/lines:** `outline_agent.py:1398-1402` — the checklist checks only `(a) exhaustive coverage … (b) information density … (c) compute need`; `:1276-1279` — `"the gap ledger is CURRENTLY EMPTY … Treat that as strong evidence the outline is adequate"`; `outline_revise.py:550-571` — a complete, validated `split` implementation with title-collision and floor guards that no detector ever systematically triggers.
- **Problem:** On a saturated corpus (this run) the ledger empties, the decide loop is *instructed* to idle, and the 11 turns end at `finish_outline` without ever rebalancing sections. Result: "Industry and Occupational Case Studies" with 93 ev_ids next to "Policy Implications" with 18 — the imbalance the reader sees as one bloated paragraph vs. one thin one. The checklist has no structural detector, and `_decide`'s rules (`:1289-1303`) never suggest `update_outline` for oversized sections.
- **Fix:** Add a deterministic (never-LLM) structural detector to `_run_checklist`'s trigger points: any section whose `len(ev_ids)` exceeds a configured multiple of the median gets a `needed_kind="structure"` todo, routed to `update_outline` with a `split` op (machinery already exists and is validated at `outline_revise.py:218`, `:550`). Symmetric: a below-floor section gets a merge-or-enrich todo. This spends already-budgeted turns on the report's shape instead of idling.
- **RACE impact:** Readability ↑ (balanced sections), Comprehensiveness ↑ (split facets get their own headings), Insight ↔.
- **Infra:** in-govkit; `needed_kind="structure"` is descriptive; multiplier + floor via resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented; ledger todos already checkpoint via `revision_audit`.

### 5. Seed assignment starves framing sections and floods bodies — no per-section ev budget, and "MAY appear in MULTIPLE sections" is uncapped
- **File/lines:** `multi_section_generator.py:3146-3176` (large-pool path: title-only digests — `summary_blocks.append(f"{ev_id} [{tier}] | title: {title_clean}")`), `:1625` (`"Evidence IDs MAY appear in MULTIPLE sections … do NOT artificially partition"`), run artifact: Introduction holds **180 ev_ids**, Cross-Study Synthesis 17, Conclusions 14.
- **Problem:** Planning 1069 rows from titles alone with no per-section budget yields catch-all assignment (Introduction as dump), and the framing sections that most reward *synthesis* (Synthesis, Conclusions) get the fewest sources. The compose writer then has 180 title-only-selected rows to open the report with — one reason the intro is a 700-word wall.
- **Fix:** Add per-role ev budgets to the facet prompt (framing roles: N_anchor high-tier rows; thematic bodies: N_core rows, overflow explicitly deferred to enrichment routing) with N values from central config; and in the skeleton addendum, state that the OPEN section takes *foundational/overview* anchors only. Pure prompt+config change at the seed.
- **RACE impact:** Readability ↑, Insight ↑ (synthesis sections get the cross-cutting evidence they need), Comprehensiveness ↔ (overflow still routed, not dropped).
- **Infra:** in-govkit; budgets via resolve()+config_defaults.py (replacing nothing hardcoded — adding seats); documented; no checkpoint shape change.

### 6. No `reorder` op exists — the agent cannot fix section order even if it wanted to
- **File/lines:** `outline_revise.py:41` — `_VALID_OPS = frozenset({"keep", "merge", "split", "retitle", "reassign", "add"})`; assembly order is "original order minus removed, then appended new" (`:631-637`), so every `add`/enrichment section lands last regardless of role.
- **Problem:** Structural ordering (finding 2's symptom) is unreachable by the revision machinery; an `add`ed thematic section will always render after Conclusions.
- **Fix:** Add a validated `reorder` op (list of existing titles; reject unknown/incomplete permutations; deferred under `required_titles` lock), plus role-aware default placement for `add` (insert before the synthesis-role section rather than append).
- **RACE impact:** Readability ↑ (coherent narrative order), enabling fix for #2.
- **Infra:** in-govkit; plain name `reorder`; no tunables; documented; deterministic apply keeps checkpoint semantics.

### 7. Infra rule 3 violations: bare `os.getenv`/inline literals throughout the outline subsystem (central config bypassed)
- **File/lines (exact quotes):**
  - `outline_agent.py:1256` — `core_threshold=_env_int("PG_OUTLINE_DECIDE_CORE_THRESHOLD", 60)`
  - `outline_agent.py:1330` — `timeout=_env_int("PG_OUTLINE_DECIDE_WALL_SECONDS", 660)`
  - `outline_agent.py:1519` — `for line in screened[:12]:` (hard cap on checklist findings, no seat at all)
  - `outline_agent.py:2481-2483` — `_env_float("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_MIN_FRAC", 0.05)`, `_env_int("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_MAX_NEW", 3)`, `_env_float("PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR_COVER", 0.5)`
  - `_fold_in.py:113` — `_env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200)`
  - `outline_toolkit.py:98,414` — `max_chars: int = 4000`, `max_chars: int = 20000`; `:132` `top_k: int = 8`; `:202` `max_rows: int = 200`; `:492` `outlier_ratio: float = 100.0`
  - display caps: `outline_agent.py:827` `kept_rows[:10]`, `:908` `members[:15]`, `:2274` `[:40]`, toolkit `:314` `ev_ids[:30]`
- **Problem:** The whole subsystem runs on module-local `_env_*` helpers with inline literals instead of `resolve()` + `config_defaults.py` — the exact pattern the infra rules reject. `screened[:12]` in particular silently discards checklist findings past 12 with no seat and no disclosure of the truncation.
- **Fix:** Migrate every seat above into `config_defaults.py` and read via `resolve()`; give `screened[:12]` a named seat and disclose when truncation fires.
- **RACE impact:** indirect (tunability for A/B of findings 1–5); compliance prerequisite for every other fix here.
- **Infra:** this *is* rules 1–5 remediation; document the seat table; no checkpoint impact.

---

**#1 highest-confidence change:** Finding 1 — add `subsections` (+ per-section `layout_hint`) to `SectionPlan` and the facet outline prompt, rendered as `###` headings/tables by compose. It attacks the single weakest measured dimension (Readability 0.3981) through the one stage that plans document shape, is purely upstream, and every large section in this run (60–180 ev_ids) is already carrying the evidence to fill 3–4 subsections each.

**Bottom line:** the outline stage plans a flat bag of 12 titles over ~60% of the corpus and idles its 11 agent turns; giving it hierarchy, a structural gap detector, role-aware placement of the 430-row residual, and enforcement of the task's own journal-only instruction converts existing evidence into the structured, instruction-compliant report the judge is scoring for — no new retrieval and no post-generation gate required.</result>

content.text: This is the smoking gun. The FACT extractor's LLM (gpt-5.4-mini) stopped after only 4,023 of 78,777 characters — 5.1% of the report. It extracted 13 facts (11 distinct refs [1]-[13]), all from the Introduction, then emitted the closing `]` of the JSON array and quit. The report actually carries 111 distinct `[N]` markers over 79KB, but FACT only "saw" the first 5%. The 79KB single-shot report is too long for the extractor to process in one pass. Let me confirm the extractor has no chunking (unlike clean_article.py which does).

content.text:
The correlation is decisive and non-monotonic in exactly the diagnostic way:

- polaris_step3_control at **29,240 chars → 110 facts extracted** (our own pipeline, shorter report)
- gemini at 55,427 → 81, fable5 at 31,053 → 58
- **faithoff_t72 at 78,777 chars → only 13 facts**

Our t72 report is the LONGEST (79KB) yet yields the FEWEST extracted facts. The extractor is a single-shot LLM call with no chunking; past ~55-60KB it truncates its JSON output after the first few thousand characters. Our own step3_control run (29KB) extracted 110 — proving the pipeline CAN cite densely; the 79KB report simply overflows the extractor. This is the root cause of "207 sentences → 11 citations."

3722	10. Multi-source citation: when MULTIPLE evidence rows independently support the same claim, cite ALL of them. Example: "the measure shifted the outcome by 2.0-2.4 points across independent analyses [ev_012][ev_034][ev_055]." Synthesize converging sources into each sentence to raise citation density where the evidence supports it.
3723	"""
3724	
3725	
3726	# I-ready-014 (#1083): anti-overcomplication / sharp-reporter concision.
3727	# The two section templates above push HARD toward MATCHING GPT-5.4 / Gemini DR
3728	# length + citation density (rule #8 "match that depth", rule #10 "50-200
3729	# citations", the Mechanism "TARGET 20-35 sentences"). The 2026 literature
3730	# (verbosity-compensation / length-controlled eval) says the opposite: front-
3731	# load the single decision-relevant finding and EARN length with distinct facts,
3732	# not sentence count. This block builds CONCISE variants of each template that
3733	# (1) prepend a front-loading directive and (2) REPLACE the length-maximizing
3734	# language with information-density language. The variants are selected ONLY when
3735	# the env flag `PG_ANTI_VERBOSITY` is truthy. Flag OFF -> the selector returns the
3736	# ORIGINAL template OBJECT unchanged (byte-identical, identity-equal). This is a
3737	# PROMPT-TEXT change ONLY: it never touches strict_verify / provenance tokens /
3738	# the 4-role seam / evidence selection. The multi-source-citation behavior (cite
3739	# ALL ev_ids that support a claim) is a CITATION rule, not a length rule, and is
3740	# preserved verbatim — only the "match GPT/Gemini density / 50-200" length-bias
3741	# clause is dropped.

3745	    "FRONT-LOADING (inverted pyramid): the FIRST sentence of this section must "
3746	    "state the single most decision-relevant finding — the direct answer to the "
3747	    "section's focus — and carry its [ev_XXX] marker. Do NOT open with "

content.text:
# FABLE REVIEW — RACE-MAXING AUDIT (task 72, RACE 0.4486)

## THE "207 SENTENCES → 11 CITATIONS" CLUE — SOLVED (this is not a compose bug; it is a report-length bug that starves the FACT extractor)

I traced the FACT pipeline directly (`third_party/deep_research_bench/results/fact/faithoff_t72_fact/extracted.jsonl` + `utils/extract.py`). Findings:

- The composed prose actually carries **111 distinct `[N]` citation markers** over 79KB (`compose_summary.json: distinct_bib_markers_in_prose=111`). Citations are NOT scarce in the report.
- FACT's extractor (`utils/extract.py`, single-shot LLM call, model `gpt-5.4-mini`) extracted only **13 facts, all from the first 4,023 of 78,777 chars (5.1% of the report)** — every ref_idx is 1–13, all inside the Introduction. It emitted the closing `]` and stopped. `extract.py` has **no chunking** (unlike `utils/clean_article.py:68-191`, which recursively splits on truncation).
- The size correlation is decisive and non-monotonic in the diagnostic direction: our own shorter run `polaris_step3_control` (29,240 chars) → **110 facts**; gemini (55KB) → 81; fable5 (31KB) → 58; **faithoff_t72 (78,777 chars, longest) → 13**. The longest report yields the fewest extracted facts.
- Root cause of the bloat: our report is 9,975 words vs step3_control's 3,914 — the extra length is the **"Additional Corroborated Findings" residual dump (801 words)** plus a **147-line reference block that carries `(tier …)` labels on every line** (2,151 words of References). That pushes the article past the extractor's single-pass ceiling.

Implication: the single fastest FACT-volume win is to **cut report length back under ~30–35KB** (kill the residual dump section, shrink/deduplicate the reference block) — the pipeline already citation-densely at that size (110 extracted in step3_control). Growing "distinct sources" is real and upstream, but the *measured* 11 is an artifact of overflowing FACT, not under-citing.

---

## RANKED FINDINGS

### #1 — Report exceeds the FACT extractor's single-pass window → 11 measured citations. Subsystem: COMPOSE/assembly.
- **file:line:** `scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:431-447` (assembly appends residual section + 147-line tier-labeled References); residual title at `weighted_enrichment.py:1353` (`_ENRICHMENT_RESIDUAL_TITLE = "Additional Corroborated Findings"`).
- **Problem:** 78,777-char report; `extract.py` reads it in ONE LLM call with no chunk fallback and truncates its JSON output at ~5%. Report is 2.5× longer than our own 110-citation step3 run.
- **Fix:** (a) drop the residual dump section from the judged body (route its baskets into the thematic sections — see #4 — instead of a trailing keep-all bucket); (b) render References as `[N] Author/Title — URL` with NO `(tier …)` label (`:446-447`); (c) dedup bibliography by URL (see #5). Target < 35KB. All upstream/formatting — no filtering gate.
- **Expected RACE/FACT impact:** FACT citation volume **11 → ~90-110** (matches step3_control at this pipeline). Readability ↑ (report stops ending in a leftover-dump + tier chrome).
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit driver; plain names; length target + `(tier)`-render toggle via resolve()+config_defaults.py (currently hardcoded at `:446`); document in assembly contract; checkpoint-neutral (assembly is post-checkpoint).

### #2 — Every section is space-joined into ONE ~517-word paragraph; prompts forbid all structure. Subsystem: COMPOSE. (Weakest dimension: Readability 0.3981)
- **file:line:** render join at `provenance_generator.py:5121` — `findings_para = " ".join(findings_lines)` (only `\n\n` is the Limitations pass-through at `:5122-5124`). Prompt contract at `multi_section_generator.py:3719-3720` (field-agnostic template used for this task): rule 7 *"Just the paragraph body"*, rule 8 *"Target 10-18 sentences"*. Measured: 12 flat H2 sections, ZERO `###`/tables/lists/bold, mean 35 words/sentence, p90 53.
- **Problem:** singular "the paragraph" × 10-18 sentences = a 500-800-word wall by construction; even if the writer inserted breaks, the join destroys them. The RACE Readability rubric explicitly scores *"formatting, headings, lists, emphasis … visual elements"* (`prompt/score_prompt_en.py:189-192`). Top run fable5 (Readability 0.5262) uses 14 table rows, 18 bullets, 104 bolds, 5 `###`, 73-word paragraphs.
- **Fix:** (a) join sentence-groups with `\n\n` (carry a paragraph index recorded at `split_into_sentences`, or break every N sentences via `resolve("PG_RENDER_PARA_MAX_SENTENCES")`); (b) rewrite rule 7 to *"2-4 short paragraphs; bold each paragraph's key finding; a short bulleted list when enumerating ≥3 parallel items."* Ships as a config-selected prompt variant. Both must land together or the resolver re-flattens.
- **Expected RACE impact:** Readability ↑↑ (largest single lever), Comprehensiveness ↑ (facets become visible).
- **Infra 1-5:** in-govkit; descriptive flag; break-cadence via resolve()+config_defaults.py; document prompt variant; checkpoint-safe (render layer).

### #3 — The outline data model has no hierarchy field → flat 12-section bag. Subsystem: OUTLINE.
- **file:line:** `multi_section_generator.py:1216-1238` (`SectionPlan` = `title/focus/ev_ids` only); facet prompt `:1616-1641` asks for `title` (≤8 words) + one-sentence `focus`, nothing structural; skeleton addendum `:966-974` constrains roles/order but zero depth.
- **Problem:** the planner cannot *plan* sub-structure, so sections with 60-180 ev_ids (Introduction=179, Case Studies=93) render as one wall each. This is the upstream root of #2.
- **Fix:** add `subsections: list[dict]` (`{title,focus,ev_ids}`) + optional `layout_hint` (`"prose"|"comparison_table"|"enumerated_findings"`) to `SectionPlan`; require 2-4 sub-facets for any section over a config ev_id threshold; compose renders `###`/tables from the plan. `dataclasses.asdict` already serializes new fields (checkpoint-safe).
- **Expected RACE impact:** Readability ↑↑, Comprehensiveness ↑, Insight ↔.
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; plain names; threshold via resolve()+config_defaults.py; document outline schema; checkpoint-safe.

### #4 — ~40% of the corpus is dumped into "Additional Corroborated Findings" rendered AFTER Conclusions. Subsystem: OUTLINE/COMPOSE.
- **file:line:** `weighted_enrichment.py:1353`; plan appended last at `multi_section_generator.py:10718` (`plans.extend(_wfe_plans)`); run artifact `multi_section_outline.json` residual carries **433 ev_ids** with `focus == "Additional Corroborated Findings"` (i.e. NO analytical directive for the largest section). Report order ends: Conclusions → this dump → Limitations → References.
- **Problem:** the thematic outline assigned ~640 ev_ids; the rest fell to a keep-all bucket that reads as a broken grab-bag and leaks pipeline vocabulary into the judged artifact. Also inflates length (feeds #1).
- **Fix:** (a) route orphan baskets to their best-matching thematic section by matching against section **focus** text (not just title tokens; `min_overlap` via config) so the residual shrinks; (b) split any remainder through the existing deterministic theme clusterer (`outline_agent.py:2096 _derive_theme_coverage_sections`) into 2-3 *titled* thematic sections placed BEFORE synthesis/conclusion roles; (c) give it a real `focus`. Pure placement — keep-all preserved.
- **Expected RACE impact:** Instruction-Following ↑ (reads as the requested literature review), Readability ↑, Comprehensiveness ↔.
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; titles derived from corpus terms; overlap/threshold via resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented; deterministic/checkpoint-safe.

### #5 — Bibliography numbers keyed by evidence_id, not by work → 147 entries for 113 URLs (24 URLs cited under 2-3 different `[N]`). Subsystem: COMPOSE.
- **file:line:** `provenance_generator.py:4735-4737` (`_num_for` increments per new ev_id); `multi_section_generator.py:7960-7971` (`_merge_bibliographies` keys `seen` on `evidence_id`, never URL). The only URL dedup (`compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:150-167`) is gated behind `--gate-artifact`, which the champion run did NOT pass, and it doesn't renumber prose anyway.
- **Problem:** same URL → multiple numbers (e.g. Brookings [104] & [105]; WEF PDF [106] & [107]; a World Bank PDF as [6][7][8]). Splits one work's citations across numbers, bloats References (feeds #1), and reads as sloppiness to the judge.
- **Fix:** key `seen` by normalized URL (fallback ev_id) in `_merge_bibliographies`; build `ev_to_global` in `_remap_section_markers_to_global:7987` via the URL group so all mirror ev_ids map to one global `[N]`. Shrinks References ~23%.
- **Expected RACE impact:** Readability ↑, and consolidates per-source citation counts (helps FACT dedup stage).
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; no new tunables; document dedup key; checkpoint-safe (post-verify remap).

### #6 — Run-on sentences are prompt-mandated; the concise variant is default-OFF. Subsystem: COMPOSE. (Readability)
- **file:line:** `multi_section_generator.py:3576-3577` (M-32/M-38 force ≥3 frame elements per study sentence → 40+ word sentences) and `:3573` M-27 packs multiple sources per sentence. The corrective `_FRONT_LOADING_DIRECTIVE`/concise variant (`:3744-3763`) is gated on `PG_ANTI_VERBOSITY`, which `config_defaults.py:75` registers as `''` (OFF) — confirmed OFF in the t72 run.
- **Problem:** mean 35 / p90 53 words-per-sentence; malformed chains like `[24].[10]` and `.[83].[24]` appear where frame-packing collides with citation resolution (10 `(also mirrored)` annotations leak into judged prose too — `provenance_generator.py:5104-5105`).
- **Fix:** default the concise variant ON (`PG_ANTI_VERBOSITY`); add a cadence rule (≤25 words/sentence average; split frame elements across two sentences — rule 12c already licenses anaphoric inheritance). Suppress `(also mirrored)` from judged prose (keep it in provenance telemetry only).
- **Expected RACE impact:** Readability ↑, Insight ↑ (front-loaded findings).
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; flag flip in config_defaults.py; document; checkpoint-neutral.

### #7 — Task's "only … high-quality, English-language journal articles" instruction is planned then overridden. Subsystem: OUTLINE. (Instruction-Following 0.4455)
- **file:line:** `reasoning_trace.jsonl` seed-outline call reasons *"only include … journal articles … safest is T1 and T2"*; overridden by facet prompt `:1636` (*"a lower-tier source … STILL earns a place"*) + route-all keep-all. Final bibliography: **T5+T6+T7+UNKNOWN = 65 of 147**, including `[100] 7 Best Medical Transcriptionist Job Tips — phoenixvirtualstaff.com (tier T6)`. RQ is quoted verbatim in the judge's prompt.
- **Fix:** derive a `source_eligibility` field at outline seed time by mechanical keyword detection ("journal articles"/"peer-reviewed"/"academic research") on the RQ; ineligible rows stay in the pool/disclosures but are withheld from section `ev_ids` (citation surface). Upstream assignment enforcing the *user's own instruction* — not a post-generation filter. (Generalization of the proven Rank12 source-eligibility firewall, 43.3%→72.8% tier compliance.)
- **Expected RACE impact:** Instruction-Following ↑↑, Readability ↑ (References stop advertising blogs), FACT unharmed.
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; plain name; detection patterns/tier map via resolve()+config_defaults.py; documented; checkpoints with existing `build_cp4_payload`.

### #8 — Tier classifier is a clinical-only taxonomy → 29% UNKNOWN on an economics corpus, down-ranks real authorities. Subsystem: FETCH.
- **file:line:** `tier_classifier.py:31-48` (T1 defined as RCT/cohort), `:2316-2325` (no-match → UNKNOWN); UNKNOWN sorts LAST in selection (`evidence_selector.py:31-34`). Same URL gets different tiers on different rows (WEF PDF as UNKNOWN and T6).
- **Fix:** add non-clinical rule families (WEF/OECD/ILO/IMF→T3; NBER/SSRN/university WPs→T4-equiv; bank/consultancy→T5) via config domain lists; add per-URL canonical-tier resolution (best-confidence wins) before banking.
- **Expected RACE impact:** Comprehensiveness/perceived-quality ↑; supports #7 (correct eligibility).
- **Infra 1-5:** govkit; domain lists via config_defaults.py; documented; checkpoint-safe.

### #9 — Live discovery strangled by dark defaults (raises the ceiling for live runs; t72 used a prebuilt corpus so this did not bind the scored run). Subsystem: SEARCH/FETCH.
- **file:line:** split-brain flag `live_retriever.py:495-504` reads `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` raw (UNSET→False→hard-drop) while `config_defaults.py:827` registers `'on'` and `evidence_selector` treats UNSET as ON — so starved/landing rows are hard-dropped at fetch on any run that doesn't export the var. Also: `PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY` (`:851`) is an unregistered bare `os.getenv` defaulting to ONE page (~10/query); winning adaptive query-gen `PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER` OFF (`config_defaults.py:636`); no Scholar vertical on the live path (`serper_client.scholar()` exists but only legacy); `frame_fetcher.py:1033` hardcodes `_M66_CONTENT_CAP = 25000` (the exact truncation live_retriever raised to 300K).
- **Fix:** route `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` through `resolve()` (UNSET→ON, + parity test); register `PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY` (~60) and flip `PG_SERPER_STOP_ON_ZERO_NEW`→`1`; enable FS-Researcher query-gen; add a `serper_scholar` engine; register `PG_FRAME_CONTENT_CAP` = 300000.
- **Expected RACE/FACT impact:** distinct citable sources ↑, T1/T2 mix ↑ → Comprehensiveness/Insight ↑ on live runs.
- **Infra 1-5:** these ARE rule-3 remediation (resolve()+config_defaults.py); document flag semantics; parity test; checkpoint-safe.

---

## #1 HIGHEST-CONFIDENCE CHANGE
**Cut the judged report below the FACT extractor's single-pass window** — remove the "Additional Corroborated Findings" residual dump from the body (route its 433 ev_ids into thematic sections, #4), dedup the bibliography by URL (#5), and strip the `(tier …)` labels from References (#1c). Our own `polaris_step3_control` run at 29KB already extracts **110 citations** through this identical pipeline; the 79KB t72 report extracts 13 purely because it overflows `extract.py`. This one change moves FACT citation volume ~11 → ~100 and simultaneously lifts Readability by ending the report on Conclusions instead of a leftover dump — no retrieval work, no gate.

## BOTTOM LINE
We are not under-citing and we are not under-fetching in the scored path — we are writing one giant unstructured 79KB wall that overflows the FACT extractor and reads flat to the RACE judge; shrink it, structure it (subsections/tables/short paragraphs), dedup it by URL, and honor the task's journal-only instruction, and both the citation-volume and Readability gaps close from purely upstream, non-filtering changes.
